participants.reasoning: The extracted participant description matches the official eligibility criteria and baseline characteristics closely, with only minor wording differences and no substantive discrepancies.
participants.extracted: Adults living in the Canterbury region of New Zealand with primary knee osteoarthritis causing moderate to severe pain, identified from orthopaedic services, primary care, and public advertising. Eligible participants had knee pain plus at least 3 of the American College of Rheumatology clinical criteria (age > 50 years, morning stiffness < 30 minutes, crepitus, bony tenderness, no palpable warmth), a WOMAC pain score of at least 20/50 at the study knee, and a stable analgesic regimen for the prior 2 months. Exclusion criteria included prior joint replacement in the study knee, recent intra-articular steroid injection, secondary OA from inflammatory arthritis, septic arthritis or trauma, hypersensitivity to nortriptyline or prior adverse reaction to any tricyclic antidepressant, current use of nortriptyline or other antidepressants, amiodarone or domperidone, recent myocardial infarction, heart block, postural hypotension, pregnancy, hyperthyroidism or phaeochromocytoma under investigation or treatment, history of epilepsy or seizure, bipolar disorder or mania, increased intra-ocular pressure or angle-closure glaucoma, chronic constipation, and urinary retention.

interventions.reasoning: The extracted intervention description matches the official details on nortriptyline dose, oral daily administration, placebo comparator, and the 14-week titration plus maintenance schedule.
interventions.extracted: Participants in the active arm receive nortriptyline, a tricyclic antidepressant, added to usual care and titrated over an 8-week blinded dose-adjustment period. All participants begin with one 25 mg capsule at night, with dose adjustments every 2 weeks based on pain relief and tolerability up to a maximum of 100 mg daily (4 capsules). After dose titration, participants continue at their maximum tolerated or effective dose for 6 weeks, for a total 14-week treatment period. The comparator is identical placebo capsules administered using the same titration and follow-up schedule, with both groups allowed to continue their usual analgesics during the study.

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes include several measures not reported in the official outcomes (global assessment, SF-36, analgesic use, CTCAE adverse events, and tricyclic side effects), while the official record instead includes serious adverse effects and study withdrawals.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure pain at 14 weeks as the primary outcome, assessed as the mean WOMAC pain subscale score (11-point numerical rating scale; range 0 to 50) adjusted for baseline pain. Secondary outcomes at 14 weeks included physical function measured with the WOMAC function subscale, participant-rated global assessment measured on a visual analogue scale (VAS), treatment response defined by the OARSI/OMERACT-OARSI responder criteria, quality of life measured using the 36-Item Short-Form Health Survey (SF-36), participant-recorded NSAID and other analgesic use during the final 2 weeks of the study period, adverse events coded using the Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE), and tricyclic adverse effects measured with the Antidepressant Side-Effect Checklist.
